tweets.text:
the trust issue on $PUMP is really the entire discount to the trade because ppl legitimately dont even believe their revenue numbers are real thats how bad it is

nobody ever mentioned if hyperliquid's revenues were real or not when it went down -70% to $10 while doing buybacks, but the discussion around pump is almost entirely negative because of the negative bias around memecoins & team's lack of comms

hyperliquid's revenue is easy for CT traders to understand because CT natives all trade perps and understand how exchanges work, in comparison very little of the large accounts on CT trade microcaps on https://t.co/1Sc1wp5IVE so they cannot grasp how they're still making money in a bear market

the trend on revenues of hyperliquid & pump are almost identical
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
full $PUMP thesis 
1. supply: biggest unlock couldn't send it lower 

2. moat: robinhood chain rugs/launchpad problems have proven the enduring value of pump's multichain rug-free guarantee. first app to RH chain trading, they want to compete w fomo 

3. KOLs: every coin needs a https://t.co/XKeh7mYFZw

im a ta trader before anything else tho and the long term support breakdown + reclaim was clear long esp w no sell pressure post unlocks day, i expected it to continue its downtrend and it didnt

thread_tweets.text:
i said similarly about robinhood when the stock was trading around $8 at the bottom bc i was mad they delisted solana at the lows, ended up buying at $14 when i realized my negative bias was probably consensus & actually a bullish catalyst if they continued to execute as a platform

if pump's revenue numbers are real & price is down bc ppl dont believe the numbers & bc they dont trust team, then its an asymmetric buying opportunity, especially if they actually start listening to the community bc theyll fix the trust issues and their revenue numbers would also likely improve, it trades at like 2x p/e while hype trades at like 20x p/e

letting your emotions get in the way of a trade is how you lose money

+ $ANSEM is the largest coin on pump, & the more ppl using their app the more ppl who also likely end up buying $ANSEM

but in all seriousness yea red ppl winning would be better for crypto but even if they dont we had the greatest bull market of all time in 2020/21 when the blue ppl were in control - wouldnt overindex on that one data point

An excellent addition for investment decisions as well.

yo @blknoiz06, i know you're bullish on crypto here, and i agree, it looks like we're starting to bottom, prob a decent spot to take a swing, but i really feel like until we get the results from the midterms, we won't see any real money flowing in.  and even then, prob won't catch a strong bid unless the republicans "win" the midterms.

really feel like the fact that trump made so much money in crypto now makes it even more partisan, and crypto will suffer if dems gain control in the short term.  curious your thoughts on this.
